title: Gothic 1

notes:
Since many people have trouble running the game. I've decided to put detailed solution to run the game at good performance. You need wine and winetricks installed on your machine in addition to Steam.
1. Install the game and setup desired language in Steam(Steam->Library->Gothic->Properties->Language).
2.Run the game -> it crashes.
3.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/65540/pfx/ winetricks directmusic"
4.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/65540/pfx/ winecfg"
5.Go to Libraries tab, in existing overrides search for 'dsound', select it and press remove button.
6.Run the game -> it runs, but probably it won't load at optimal screen resolution.
7.In gothic.ini: "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini" set your screen resolution, since this will revert every time you reload the game, I came with workaround.
8.In your Launch Options(Steam->Library->Gothic->Properties->Set Launch Options) paste: "awk -v var="zVidResFullscreenX" -v new_val="1920" 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="="}match($1, "^\\s*" var "\\s*") {$2=""new_val}1' ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini > tmp.txt && mv tmp.txt ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini && awk -v var="zVidResFullscreenY" -v new_val="1080" 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="="}match($1, "^\\s*" var "\\s*") {$2=""new_val}1' ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini > tmp.txt && mv tmp.txt ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini && awk -v var="zVidResFullscreenBPP" -v new_val="32" 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="="}match($1, "^\\s*" var "\\s*") {$2=""new_val}1' ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini > tmp.txt && mv tmp.txt ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Gothic/system/Gothic.ini && %command%", this will basically restore FullHD config for you.
9.Have fun!
10.Don't bork the game without trying this.
title: Gothic 1

cpu: AMD A8-3870 APU with Radeon HD Graphics

title: Gothic 1

notes:
Like several users mentioned before, those steps helped me to run the game perfectly: 
(0. I had disabled Steam Overlay)

1. Install the game (and Proton 4.11-3) 
2. Run following command inside a terminal: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/65540/pfx/ winetricks directmusic" 
The Path may vary, depends on where you have installed your games.
4.Run the command: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/65540/pfx/ winecfg" 5.Go to Libraries tab, in existing overrides search for 'dsound', select it and press remove button.
6. Start the game and change all settings to your needs ( I also changed audio to "Miles Fast" Provider) 
and play the game for a few seconds. 
7. After a new restart - everything was fine. (No Audio lag anymore)

What was not working: Videos Sequences
title: Gothic 1
